# S3

| | |
| --- | --- |
| **Import** | `@harryy/ai-tools/s3` |
| **Kind** | **vendor** (`src/vendors/s3`) |
| **Module id** | `s3` |
| **Client** | `S3Client` |
| **Transport** | aws4fetch SigV4 via `AwsService` |

S3 and S3-compatible object store (AWS S3, R2 S3 endpoint, MinIO, …).

## Auth

```ts
{
  access_key_id: string
  secret_access_key: string
  region: string
  bucket: string
  endpoint?: string        // e.g. https://<account_id>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com
  session_token?: string
}
```

## Host / tools

```ts
import { S3Client, s3Module } from '@harryy/ai-tools/s3'
import { withAuth } from '@harryy/ai-tools/core'

const s3 = new S3Client({
  access_key_id: '…',
  secret_access_key: '…',
  region: 'auto',
  bucket: 'my-bucket',
  endpoint: 'https://….r2.cloudflarestorage.com',
})

withAuth(s3Module, { /* same auth */ })
```

## Tool ids

`s3-list-objects`, `s3-get-object`, `s3-put-object`, `s3-delete-object`, `s3-head-object`, `s3-copy-object`, `s3-create-signed-url`, `s3-create-multipart-upload`, `s3-upload-part`, `s3-complete-multipart-upload`, `s3-abort-multipart-upload`.

For a multi-provider seam, use [@harryy/ai-tools/storage](../modules/storage.md) with `provider: 's3'`.
